Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails for Patricia Langford's recent message to Catalina using search_emails (query: "Patricia Langford" or "Canopy" or similar). Find her April 25 email (email_patricia_canopy_market_anxiety_20260425) expressing concern about PathFinder Logistics making an aggressive push into Chicago-area healthcare accounts after running into Pam Kowalski from NorthWind at a networking event. Patricia asks whether this affects MoveOps' ability to support Canopy Health's upcoming moves. Separately, search for Patricia's email to Mina Hashimoto about the Dr. Anand Mehta executive relocation to discover that Patricia bypassed Catalina and went directly to Mina for VIP handling of the CMO relocation.
OE 2: Search emails for Sam Delgado's messages to Catalina using search_emails (query: "Sam Delgado" or "Nadia" or "Diego" or similar). Find his relocation request emails (pivotpoint_dual_reloc_request and pivotpoint_dual_reloc_followup, approximately April 8) detailing two new Austin relocations: Nadia Okonjo (H-1B visa holder, USCIS 10-day address-change requirement, attorney Lorraine Pham at Pham & Associates) and Diego Castañeda (CNC mill requiring custom crating/climate-controlled transport, Brooklyn lease ending April 15, pre-move inspection requested April 10-11, equipment insured for $42,000).
OE 3: Search Slack for Canopy surcharge discussions using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "surcharge" or "Canopy" or "INV-2026-0401" or similar). Find Hana Kim's April 4 post in #finance (C005) detailing invoice INV-2026-0401 for Canopy Health ($9,210 total, due May 4). The post flags $2,650 in non-standard surcharges pending client approval: $850 customs broker (Kovacs, Nashville→Toronto), $1,200 cold-chain freight (Webb, Detroit→Chicago), $600 temp storage (Okonkwo, Philadelphia→Denver). Also find Marcus Thorne's reply noting the April 7 surcharge approval deadline and requesting Catalina escalate by phone if Patricia hasn't responded.
OE 4: Search Slack for PivotPoint or USCIS discussions using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Nadia" or "USCIS" or "PivotPoint" or similar). Find relevant Slack messages in #operations (C006) about PivotPoint relocations. Separately, search Airtable for Nadia Okonjo's relocation record (recNadiaOkonjoSFAustin), which contains H-1B visa details, the USCIS 10-day address-change filing window, and attorney Lorraine Pham's contact information at Pham & Associates. The Airtable record and Sam's emails together establish the visa deadline urgency and the gap in scheduling Diego's CNC pre-move inspection.
OE 5: Search emails for Greg Pallone's vendor rate notification using search_emails (query: "Greg Pallone" or "Swift" or "rate increase" or similar). Find Greg Pallone's email (greg_swift_rate_increase_20260409) announcing Swift Freight Solutions' 15% rate increase on all climate-controlled shipments effective May 1, 2026, plus a new $500 DOT hazardous materials compliance surcharge. Cross-reference with Diego's CNC mill line item in QuickBooks invoice INV-2026-0315 (climate-controlled freight surcharge: $850) to determine the pricing impact on PivotPoint's invoice.
OE 6: Search CRM for Canopy Health company record using crm_search_companies (name: "Canopy Health"). CRM returns no existing records for Canopy Health. The deal and engagement data for this client is instead distributed across other services: QuickBooks (INV-2026-0401 with line-item pricing), Airtable (relocation records for Kovacs, Webb, Okonkwo), and Linear (project issues tracking the moves and Dr. Mehta executive relocation). The absence of CRM data confirms fresh engagement notes are needed.
OE 7: Search CRM for any existing Canopy Health engagements using crm_list_engagements or crm_search_companies. CRM returns no engagement records. The AM coverage situation (Patricia bypassing Catalina to contact Mina about Mehta) is discoverable through Patricia's email to Mina Hashimoto and through Linear issues where Mina is listed as Account Manager on Mehta-related tickets. The surcharge approval timeline is documented in Hana Kim's April 4 Slack post and Marcus Thorne's follow-up.
OE 8: Search CRM for PivotPoint Labs company record using crm_search_companies (name: "PivotPoint"). CRM returns no existing records for PivotPoint Labs. The deal information is instead distributed across QuickBooks (INV-2026-0315, $10,650), Airtable (recNadiaOkonjoSFAustin and recDiegoCastanedaBrooklynAustin relocation records with visa and CNC mill details), and Sam Delgado's original request emails.
OE 9: Search CRM for any existing PivotPoint Labs engagements using crm_list_engagements or crm_search_companies. CRM returns no engagement records. The full relocation details including attorney Lorraine Pham contact info and USCIS filing requirements are documented in Airtable (recNadiaOkonjoSFAustin) and Sam's emails. The absence of CRM engagement history confirms fresh notes are needed.
OE 10: Search QuickBooks for Canopy Health invoices using quickbooks_search_invoices. Find INV-2026-0401 (Id: 1009), $9,210 total, due May 4, with line items including the three pending surcharges.
OE 11: Search QuickBooks for PivotPoint Labs invoices using quickbooks_search_invoices. Find INV-2026-0315 (Id: inv_pivotpoint_april_2026_dual), $10,650 total, with line items including $1,200 custom crating and $850 climate-controlled freight surcharge for Diego's CNC mill. Compare these QuickBooks figures against Greg Pallone's email about Swift Freight Solutions' 15% rate increase plus $500 DOT surcharge (effective May 1) to assess the pricing impact on Diego's freight line item.
OE 12: Search Airtable relocation records for Canopy Health moves using airtable_search_records or airtable_list_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: Relocations). Find records for Kovacs (recKovacsNashvilleToronto), Webb (recMarcusWebbDetroitChicago), Okonkwo (recTylerOkonkwoPhillyDenver), all In Progress with coordinator assignments.
OE 13: Search Airtable relocation records for PivotPoint moves using airtable_search_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: Relocations, field_name: Company, value: PivotPoint Labs). Find recNadiaOkonjoSFAustin (In Progress, Fatimah coordinator, H-1B visa notes) and recDiegoCastanedaBrooklynAustin (In Progress, Javier coordinator, CNC mill specs, pre-move inspection requested April 10-11).
OE 14: Search Linear for Canopy Health project issues using linear_list_issues (project: proj_canopy or query: "Canopy" or "Mehta"). Find Mehta tracking tickets (temporary housing, equipment chain-of-custody, household move) assigned to Fatimah Al-Rashidi as coordinator, with Mina Hashimoto listed as Account Manager in the issue descriptions, confirming the AM coverage situation.
OE 15: Search Linear for PivotPoint project issues using linear_list_issues (project: proj_pivot or query: "PivotPoint" or "Okonjo"). Determine whether a visa deadline tracking ticket already exists.
OE 16: Create a CRM engagement note using crm_create_engagement for Canopy Health (company_ids: ["company_canopy"]) documenting the AM coverage clarification (Catalina remains primary, Mina handling Mehta only), surcharge approval status ($2,650 pending since April 4), and Patricia's call request.
OE 17: Create a CRM engagement note using crm_create_engagement for PivotPoint Labs (company_ids: ["company_pivot"]) documenting the visa filing urgency, CNC inspection gap, invoice pricing discrepancy from vendor rate increase, and communication gap with Sam.
OE 18: Update Airtable relocation records using airtable_update_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_id: tblRelocations01) for any records showing outdated status. PivotPoint moves (recNadiaOkonjoSFAustin and/or recDiegoCastanedaBrooklynAustin) need status notes reflecting visa deadline urgency and inspection scheduling gap. Also evaluate Canopy records (recKovacsNashvilleToronto, recMarcusWebbDetroitChicago, recTylerOkonkwoPhillyDenver) whose move end dates have passed and update if status no longer reflects current state.
OE 19: Create a Linear issue using linear_create_issue (team: team_operations or team_relocations, title: related to Nadia Okonjo USCIS address-change filing deadline, priority: 1 or 2) tracking the visa filing deadline with attorney Lorraine Pham contact details and the 10-day filing window.
OE 20: Send an email using send_email (or reply_to_email) from catalina.dubois@moveops.com to patricia.langford@canopyhealth.io. Content confirms Catalina is still primary AM, provides surcharge breakdown with justification ($850 customs, $1,200 cold-chain, $600 storage = $2,650 total), and confirms a Thursday call.
OE 21: Send an email using send_email (or reply_to_email) from catalina.dubois@moveops.com to sam@pivotpointlabs.com. Content provides status updates on Nadia (visa filing being escalated, address confirmation in progress) and Diego (CNC inspection scheduling, pricing review underway), and acknowledges the gap in response time.
OE 22: Post a message using conversations_add_message in #operations Slack channel (channel_id: C006) updating Chloe that Catalina is back on PivotPoint and addressing the visa deadline and inspection timeline.
OE 23: Create a calendar event using calendar_add_calendar_event for Thursday April 30, 2026 afternoon with Patricia Langford as attendee, titled related to Canopy Health account review or surcharge discussion (or similar).

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

2 The Canopy Health CRM engagement note mentions that Catalina Dubois remains the primary account manager or point of contact (or similar).
outcome
